strengths: Proven data quality and CI/CD practices
weaknesses: Limited explicit years >4 in role (overall timeline compressed)
No explicit reverse ETL tools experience
Primary cloud focus on GCP; AWS/Snowflake depth less quantified
improvement_bullets: Leveraged advanced SQL queries, including window functions and CTEs, to extract and transform data from REST APIs and relational databases, building structured datasets that directly supported front-end feature development and reduced UI bugs by 60%.

Developed and maintained production-grade ELT pipelines using Python, Apache Airflow, and Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery, GCS) to ingest Federal Reserve economic data, ensuring data reliability and availability for downstream analytics.
Designed and implemented data models in a dbt star schema, optimizing BigQuery performance through partitioning and clustering, significantly reducing analyst query time.
Automated data quality validation processes using Great Expectations and deployed a CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions, ensuring robust and reliable data pipelines.

Collaborated with cross-functional stakeholders, including product managers and business teams, to translate user feedback and business requirements into production code, driving a 15% increase in feature adoption and reducing product delivery delays by 25%.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer opportunity with Figma. I am interested in this opportunity because Figma's mission to make design accessible to all deeply resonates with my passion for building tools and systems that empower others to create and innovate. What interests me about working for Figma is its innovative approach to design collaboration and its commitment to fostering a culture of continuous growth. This excites, inspires, and intrigues me because I thrive in environments that push boundaries and encourage learning.

I believe my proficiency in SQL and Python, coupled with my experience in building scalable data pipelines, would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a problem-solving mindset and a focus on delivering impactful solutions. In fact, at Haven Technologies, I extracted and transformed data from REST APIs and relational databases, building structured, clean datasets that directly supported front-end feature development and reduced UI bugs by 60%. This resulted in improved data accuracy and streamlined development processes.

Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer role at Figma would allow me to bring my expertise in building and maintaining scalable data pipelines to support Figma's core data models and enable self-service analytics. Additionally, my experience building ELT pipelines for FRED economic data enabled me to develop my data modeling and automation skills. Specifically, I modeled raw API data into a dbt star schema, reducing analyst query time via BigQuery partitioning and clustering, and automated data quality validation with Great Expectations and deployed a CI/CD pipeline via GitHub Actions.

I’m excited by the opportunity to use my data modeling skills in working on foundational data sets and specialized data sets for analytics at Figma. I know that my strong SQL and Python skills, along with my hands-on experience with dbt and Airflow, align with what it takes to succeed in the Data Engineer role.

I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,
Justin Miller
